results.final_yaml:
nika: v1
workflow: daily-jira-standup

tasks:
  - id: get_date
    exec:
      command: date +%Y-%m-%d

  - id: fetch_jira_export
    invoke:
      tool: nika:fetch
      args:
        path: ${{ jira_export_path != '' ? jira_export_path : './jira-export.json' }}

  - id: filter_open_tickets
    invoke:
      tool: nika:jq
      args:
        input: ${{ fetch_jira_export.result }}
        query: >
          [.issues[] | 
           select(.fields.status.name as $s | 
                  $s == "Open" or $s == "In Progress" or $s == "To Do") |
           {
             key: .key,
             summary: .fields.summary,
             status: .fields.status.name,
             assignee: (.fields.assignee.displayName // "Unassigned"),
             priority: .fields.priority.name
           }] |
          group_by(.assignee) |
          map({assignee: .[0].assignee, tickets: .})

  - id: generate_standup
    infer: |
      Create a brief daily standup summary from these grouped Jira tickets:
      
      ${{ filter_open_tickets.result }}
      
      Format as markdown with:
      - Header: "Daily Standup - ${{ get_date.stdout }}"
      - Team members listed with their assigned tickets
      - Each ticket: [KEY] Summary (Priority)
      - Separate "Blockers" section for Critical/High priority items
      - Keep under 200 words, suitable for quick standup reading
      
      Return only the markdown content, no additional commentary.

  - id: save_standup
    invoke:
      tool: nika:write
      args:
        path: standup-${{ get_date.stdout }}.md
        content: ${{ generate_standup.text }}

results.final_yaml:
nika: v1
workflow: test-deploy-notify

tasks:
  - id: run_tests
    exec:
      command: npm test

  - id: run_linter
    exec:
      command: npm run lint

  - id: confirm_deployment
    infer:
      prompt: "Tests and linter have completed successfully. Do you want to proceed with the deployment?"
    depends_on:
      - run_tests
      - run_linter

  - id: deploy
    exec:
      command: ./deploy.sh
    depends_on: [confirm_deployment]

  - id: notify_team_success
    invoke:
      tool: slack
      args:
        channel: team
        message: "✅ Deployment completed successfully!"
    depends_on: [deploy]

  - id: notify_team_failure
    invoke:
      tool: slack
      args:
        channel: team
        message: "❌ Deployment cancelled or failed. Manual review needed."
    on_error:
      skip: true

nika: v1
workflow: docs-build-publish-verify
env:
  DOCS_LIVE_URL: https://docs.example.com

tasks:
  - id: build_docs
    exec:
      command: npm run build
    on_error:
      fail_workflow: true

  - id: check_build_passed
    exec:
      command: test -d dist && echo "✓ Build artifacts verified"
    on_error:
      fail_workflow: true

  - id: get_human_signoff
    agent:
      prompt: |
        Build completed successfully and verified.
        Build artifacts are ready for production deployment.
        
        Please review and confirm approval to publish.

  - id: publish_docs
    exec:
      command: npm run deploy
    on_error:
      fail_workflow: true

  - id: verify_live_version
    invoke:
      tool: nika:fetch
      with:
        url: ${{ env.DOCS_LIVE_URL }}
        retryAttempts: 5
        retryDelaySeconds: 10

  - id: confirm_deployment_success
    infer:
      prompt: |
        Deployment verification complete.
        
        Summary:
        - Build: ✓ Passed
        - Review: ✓ Approved
        - Published: ✓ Deployed
        - Live verification: ✓ Confirmed
        
        Docs site is now live and verified matching the build.
